business.headline: RBI ends FEMA probe against Myntra. Here's what happenedAccording to the Enforcement Directorate (ED), the RBI passed the order on April 20, 2026 under Section 15 of FEMA after receiving a "No Objection" from the agency. As a result, the investigation against the company has been terminated.

LIC owns 10% of Rajesh Exports, shares fall 1% at closing bell amid Sebi allegationsShareholding data for the March 2026 quarter shows that Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC), the country's largest insurer and institutional investor, holds a 10.80% stake in Rajesh Exports.

Revenues are correct, says Rajesh Exports amid Sebi allegationsThe company's response after Sebi issued an interim order against Rajesh Exports and its promoter Rajesh Mehta, raising concerns about the company's financial reporting and questioning whether a large part of its reported revenues had adequate supporting documents.

Sensex, Nifty end flat ahead of RBI policy decision; Titan jumps 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 13.84 points, or 0.02%, to close at 74,360.01, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 10.95 points, or 0.05%, to settle at 23,416.55.

A gold empire, a Swiss refinery and SEBI's Rs 15.15 lakh crore allegationSebi has questioned Rajesh Exports over Rs 15.15 lakh crore of subsidiary revenue between FY21 and FY25. The interim order has intensified scrutiny of the Valcambi owner and sent its shares to the lower circuit.

India plans bond tax break to attract foreign moneyForeign investors have withdrawn nearly $28 billion from Indian equities this year. While foreign money has continued to flow into government bonds, with net investments of around $1.4 billion, policymakers appear keen to attract more stable capital into the debt market.

Why the RBI may avoid a rate cut this time: 3 things to knowAs the Reserve Bank of India prepares to announce its latest monetary policy decision, expectations of another rate cut appear to be fading. Most experts now believe the central bank will keep the repo rate unchanged and continue with a wait-and-watch approach. Here are three reasons why.

Carlsberg India strengthens board as IPO plans gather momentumAccording to officials familiar with the matter, the appointments bring expertise in consumer goods, public policy, leadership and human resources as the company prepares for the next stage of its growth.

How Sebi uncovered a Rs 15 lakh crore revenue hole at Rajesh ExportsA shareholder complaint, a Swiss refinery and years of unexplained revenues have culminated in one of the most significant accounting investigations in India's corporate history.

Sebi's Rs 15 lakh crore question: What went wrong at Rajesh Exports?Sebi's interim order has questioned whether Rajesh Exports backed its reported revenues with verifiable records. The findings have intensified scrutiny of disclosures, promoter-linked transactions and investor-facing accounts.

Gold is rising again: 3 things investors should knowGold prices are moving higher again after a period of weakness and attracting fresh buying interest. Here's what's driving the rally and what investors should keep in mind.

Rajesh Exports shares hit lower circuit as Sebi alleges 97-99% revenue inflationSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports sent the stock to a 5% lower circuit after it alleged possible revenue inflation and barred promoter Rajesh Mehta. The action has heightened investor uncertainty as the regulator has sought more records and ordered a fresh forensic audit.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran-US tensions keep Dalal Street on edgeIndian benchmark indices opened lower as West Asia tensions and foreign selling weighed on sentiment. Elevated crude prices, FPI outflows and the RBI policy outlook remain key market triggers.

Foreign investors get major capital gains tax relief from the governmentThe Centre is weighing tax changes to make Indian government bonds more attractive to foreign investors. The proposed steps are aimed at boosting inflows, supporting the rupee and easing market pressure.

Why file your ITR before July 31? These 3 benefits could helpWith the July 31 ITR deadline approaching, taxpayers have another reason to avoid delays. Filing your return on time can unlock several benefits that could prove useful throughout the year.

India responds to proposed US tariffs as trade pact negotiations continueWhile discussions over the tariff proposal continue, India and the United States are also working to finalise a broader trade agreement.

CCPA fines PhysicsWallah and McAfee for using dark patterns on digital platformsCCPA has also directed both companies to remove dark pattern practices that influenced consumer choices on their digital platforms.

Only 1% left to resolve in India- US trade pact, says US envoy Sergio GorSpeaking at Citi's 2026 India Conference on Wednesday, Gor said both countries were working to resolve the remaining "one per cent" sticking point and were hopeful of completing the agreement within the next several weeks.

Sensex ends 304 points lower as crude nears $100, IT stocks crackThe S&P BSE Sensex ended 303.67 points lower, or 0.41%, at 74,346.17, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 77.95 points, or 0.33%, to close at 23,405.60.

Big relief for airlines as govt caps jet fuel prices, clears Rs 10,000-crore fundThe decision comes amid recent developments in West Asia that have pushed up international ATF prices and increased pressure on airlines.

entertainment.headline: Chiranjeevi, proud father and actor, reviews Ram Charan's Peddi Chiranjeevi reviewed Ram Charan's sports drama Peddi and congratulated the film's cast and crew after its release. He expressed hope that the film would bring more milestones for Telugu cinema.

Kriti Sanon's trainer on how she achieved her toned look for Cocktail 2 bikini scene Karan Sawhney has detailed how Kriti Sanon prepared for her first bikini scene in Cocktail 2. He highlighted the actor's effort to achieve a fit, lean look without losing the character's glow.

Gram Chikitsalay 2: When and where to watch Amol Parashar's rural drama series Gram Chikitsalay is set to return with a second season as Dr Prabhat's journey in the village of Bhathkandi continues with new challenges. Here's everything to know about the cast, story and where to watch the rural comedy-drama.

Maa Behen actor Dharna reacts to influencers being cast for their social media reach Dharna Durga said she landed Maa Behen after completing the full audition process and later shared screen space with Madhuri Dixit. She said the experience eased doubts around influencer casting and left her impressed by Dixit's warmth.

Actor Sambhavna Seth welcomes twins, says Maha Diwali came early this year Actor Sambhavna Seth and her husband, actor-writer Avinash Dwivedi, have announced the birth of their twins. The announcement marks a joyful turn in their long journey to parenthood and drew warm wishes from fans and celebrities.

Lalit Modi confirms biopic is in the works, says Ranveer Singh wanted to play lead Lalit Modi said his biopic is in the scripting stage. He recalled Ranveer Singh flying to London to discuss playing him on screen.

Ranveer Allahbadia says life won't be same after Ujjain visit with Juhi Bhatt Ranveer Allahbadia celebrated his 33rd birthday with Juhi Bhatt in Ujjain and offered prayers at Mahakaal. The visit also came months after he publicly confirmed their relationship on Instagram.

Obsession's sleeper hit box office status: How no-star horror film stunned Indians The surprise success of Obsession proves that compelling storytelling and word-of-mouth can still outperform star power and massive marketing budgets in today's film industry.

Tom Holland explains why Spider-Man: Brand New Day faced a production delay Tom Holland has revealed the surprising behind-the-scenes issue that delayed Spider-Man: Brand New Day. The actor explains how a difficult conversation with Sony changed the film's timeline.

Pranit More under scanner: List of comedians who 'crossed a line' with humour Pranit More's recent crowd-work controversy has revived scrutiny of jokes made in live comedy settings. The backlash has also drawn attention to earlier rows involving Samay Raina, Munawar Faruqui and Kunal Kamra.

Singer Chinmayi on Delhi fire: Case will be dealt properly as victims are foreigners Singer Chinmayi Sripada reacted to the deadly Malviya Nagar fire in Delhi after 21 people died at a bed-and-breakfast property. She said the case may be handled properly because several of the victims were foreigners.

Prime Video, ZEE5, Sony LIV this week: Gullak 5, Brown, The Pyramid Scheme and more This week's OTT lineup brings a diverse mix of crime thrillers, family dramas and romances, led by Brown, Gullak Season 5 and The Pyramid Scheme. From Karisma Kapoor's gritty cop drama to the return of the beloved Mishra family, there's something for every viewer.

Why Ucha Lamba Kad 2.O cannot match Katrina Kaif's original magic Ucha Lamba Kad 2.O may bring fresh beats and a new pairing with Akshay Kumar and Disha Patani, but it also serves as a reminder of one undeniable truth: some songs are impossible to separate from the stars who made them iconic.

Aamir Khan confirms marriage to Gauri Spratt: Ready to take it to next level Aamir Khan has confirmed that he and Gauri Spratt are set to marry on July 5. The news comes months after the actor had said that marriage was not on the cards for them.

Curry Barker expands world of Obsession with new film Anything But Ghosts Curry Barker has said Anything But Ghosts is set in the same universe as Obsession. The new film carries a direct reference to Nikki's fate, linking both stories.

Calm your wild thoughts down: Nushrratt Bharuccha breaks silence on viral clip Nushrratt Bharuccha has clarified that the background audio in her viral video was of a puppy crying at a friend's house. The actor also slammed the rumours and unfounded theories that surfaced online following the clip.

Ranveer Singh is truly a Dhurandhar: Film body member Upasana Singh amid Don 3 row Upasana Singh came out in support of Ranveer Singh as the Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) withdrew its non-cooperation directive against him. Also a member of Cine & TV Artistes' Association (CINTAA), she called the actor a true Dhurandhar.

Maa Behen review: A deliciously chaotic dramedy that gets its women just rightRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026 In Maa Behen, three women are thrown into chaos after a neighbour dies in their house. The dramedy uses gossip, crime and family friction to show how society misreads women.

Jennifer Aniston looks back at ex Brad Pitt's unforgettable Friends stint Jennifer Aniston and Lisa Kudrow recalled that many Hollywood stars, including Brad Pitt, were often nervous about appearing on Friends. Reflecting on the famous guest appearances, they said even established movie stars sometimes found it difficult to adjust to the show's unique rhythm.

general news.headline: How Mamata's Trinamool unravelled at breakneck speed within 30 daysMamata Banerjee, Bengal's OG street fighter who built the TMC from scratch, faces her sternest test yet as the party teeters on the brink of disintegration following a rebellion by 60 MLAs led by Ritabrata Banerjee. The big question is whether the party can remain united after losing power for the first time since 2011.

Did Dawood Ibrahim plot IPL takeover? Lalit Modi's bombshell claimsFormer IPL chief Lalit Modi has claimed that a plot by Dawood Ibrahim to control a tournament franchise and threats over disrupted illegal betting networks ultimately forced his complete exile from cricket administration.

Will protest at Jantar Mantar even if permission denied, says cockroach partyThe Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), which announced its plan to protest at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar on Saturday, said it would go ahead with the agitation even if permission was denied to it by Delhi Police. It said it would seek permission after CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke lands in Delhi. CJP spokespersons said they planned a peaceful agitation but couldn't predict how the situation on the ground could unfold.

IndiGo to pause services to Hong Kong, Shanghai, 4 other cities from next monthIndiGo will suspend flights to six international destinations – Langkawi, Krabi, Ho Chi Minh City, Hong Kong, Shanghai and Siem Reap – from July 1 and 3 until September 30, citing weaker demand and higher operating costs despite the ongoing summer travel season.

If not youngsters, then who? Army veterans back Captain's viral chopper proposalA video of an Indian Army aviator proposing to his fiancée beside a Cheetah helicopter sparked a row on social media. Many expressed concerns over the decorum, protocol and security. However, retired top generals have defended Captain Bharat Bhardwaj, saying the gesture didn't amount to a security breach, and his expression of love shouldn't be mistaken for indiscipline.

Newsrooms can moderate and calibrate, algorithms chase engagement: Kalli Purie on AIAt SPIEF 2026, India Today Group Vice-Chairperson and Editor-in-Chief Kalli Purie underlined that AI must support, not replace, newsroom judgement. Other speakers from Russia and China echoed concerns over conscience, cybersecurity and the risks of unchecked automation.

TMC might find an unlikely saviour in Bengal. The BJP. Here's whyThe TMC's internal turmoil has delighted many in the BJP. But others in the party worry that if the Mamata Banerjee-founded party collapses entirely, the BJP might end up facing the rapidly reviving CPI(M)-led Left Front. Then there could be the Left-Congress combine too.

Semi-Finals LIVE: Maja Chwalinska vs Diana Shnaider 1st set into tie-breakFrench Open 2026 semifinals Live Updates: Diana Shnaider and Maja Chwalinska are currently locking horns in the second women's singles semif-final. Earlier, Mirra Andreeva beat Marta Kostyuk in straight sets to reach her maiden Grand Slam final.

2000 vs 2026: How the cost of essentials in India has changedA comparison of selected everyday essentials shows how prices have changed between 2000 and 2026, offering a snapshot of rising household expenses amid India's economic growth.

DMK to skip INDIA bloc meet after fallout with Congress over Tamil Nadu pollsAfter the Congress backed Vijay's TVK to form the government in Tamil Nadu, the DMK accused the grand old party of "betrayal" and "backstabbing".

He is 16. He broke into NEET and JEE portals to fix itAt just 16, Dubai-based CBSE student Rylen Anil has exposed vulnerabilities in both the NEET and JEE Advanced systems within days of each other. In an interview with India Today, he spoke about reporting the flaws, receiving a response from top officials, and why he believes young ethical hackers are helping make India's education systems more secure.

How Sebi uncovered a Rs 15 lakh crore revenue hole at Rajesh ExportsA shareholder complaint, a Swiss refinery and years of unexplained revenues have culminated in one of the most significant accounting investigations in India's corporate history.

Aamir Khan confirms marriage to Gauri Spratt: Ready to take it to next levelAamir Khan has confirmed that he and Gauri Spratt are set to marry on July 5. The news comes months after the actor had said that marriage was not on the cards for them.

Sridhar Vembu issues AI warning for students, says it can make them dumb fastZoho founder Sridhar Vembu has warned students against relying too heavily on AI tools, saying they can weaken independent thinking and learning. His comments come as rising failure rates at UC Berkeley have sparked fresh debate about AI's impact on education.

Groceries in 10 minutes, emergency services in 60: Delhi fire exposes cruel ironyDelhi fire: The rescue operation in the initial minutes at the B&B in Delhi's Malviya Nagar relied heavily on locals who risked their own safety to help those trapped inside. The incident has firmly put scrutiny on the alleged delay by emergency services.

Lalit Modi gifts his son a Ferrari every birthday. Here's whyFor Lalit Modi, a childhood longing he never fulfilled has evolved into a unique family tradition, with the businessman gifting his son a Ferrari every birthday because he never received one from his own father. His son, who received his first Ferrari at 21, now has a collection of 12.

Video: Nose gear of parked Lufthansa jet collapses at Frankfurt airportThe incident occurred at 12:45 PM (10:45 GMT), and the jet was scheduled to depart for Los Angeles as flight LH450, Lufthansa said. The flight was later cancelled, reported Flightradar24.

I finally got my passport, but I can't stop thinking about India's reputation abroadA first-time traveller says getting a passport has been overshadowed by worry over how Indian tourists are viewed abroad. The anxiety reflects a wider debate over viral incidents, perceived behaviour and the reputational cost for Indian travellers.

Monsoon fury on Friday: Heavy rain alerts for North and South IndiaThe southwest monsoon will heavy rain to southern India on Friday, June 5. Get the latest weather forecast and safety advisories for your region.

Hidden in Karnataka Cabinet, the all-powerful SiddaramaiahDK Shivakumar has become the Karnataka CM after years of posturing against his former boss and Congress veteran, Siddaramaiah. However, even as Shivakumar is the CM, it's seemingly former CM Siddaramaiah who's holding all the cards.

Peddi review: Ram Charan and the emotions hit sixes, the writing gets run outRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026Peddi movie review: Ram Charan's Peddi follows a nameless village's fight for a railway station and identity. The film lands emotionally despite visible logic gaps, weak romance writing and uneven editing.

Physically in TMC, but not mentally: Bengal MP claims more MPs will quit soonThe Trinamool Congress may be headed for a deeper crisis, with veteran party MP Sukhendu Sekhar Ray claiming that the rebellion that has rocked the party's ranks could soon spill over to the Lok Sabha.

Gurgaon doctor explains why a BMW owner earning Rs 40 lakh feels poorDr Sunny Garg recounted meeting a Gurgaon man earning Rs 40 lakh who said he felt poor. He used the case to argue that shifting comparisons and lifestyle creep are reshaping how professionals define poverty.

Indian-origin woman in UK wins Rs 85 crore in 23-year-long divorce battleWhat began as a routine divorce in London turned into a 23-year legal war after solicitor Bhadresh Gohil was convicted in a massive money-laundering case linked to a former Nigerian governor. The revelations helped Varsha Gohil reopen the settlement and eventually secure an Rs 85 crore payout.

sports.headline: Wicketkeeper-batter KS Bharat retires at 32 after falling down the pecking order KS Bharat has retired from international cricket after a seven-Test career with India. The move follows his drop in the wicketkeeping pecking order and clears the path for overseas T20 opportunities.

RCB's Jacob Bethell faces the heat as Jamieson blows England away in Lord's Test England slid to 118 for 8 on the opening day at Lord's as New Zealand's pacers exposed a brittle batting line-up. Jacob Bethell's modest return intensified questions over his stint in the IPL in the lead-up to the Test series.

Mirra Andreeva heals old wounds, downs Marta Kostyuk to reach maiden major final French Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva continued her dream run at Roland Garros, defeating Marta Kostyuk 6-1, 6-3 to reach her maiden Grand Slam final. The 19-year-old produced a composed and dominant display to end Kostyuk's 17-match clay-court winning streak.

Virat Kohli almost doesn't want the life he lives in India, says ex-RCB teammate Former England all-rounder Liam Livingstone has revealed that Virat Kohli actively seeks to maintain a low profile in India, shedding light on the sharp contrast between the superstar's ultra-competitive on-field persona and his quiet family life.

Will Rashid Khan return for Tests? Afghanistan captain stays tight-lipped IND vs AFG: Hashmatullah Shahidi declined to clarify whether Rashid Khan will play Test cricket again before Afghanistan's match against India. His absence keeps the leg-spinner's red-ball future uncertain as Afghanistan seek more regular Tests.

Patidar is a strong contender for India's T20I captaincy: Ex-India selector Saba Karim has put Rajat Patidar in the mix for India's T20I captaincy after his back-to-back IPL titles with RCB. The debate has widened ahead of the Ireland series, with selectors also considering Shreyas Iyer, Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma.

Semi-Finals LIVE: Maja Chwalinska vs Diana Shnaider 1st set into tie-break French Open 2026 semifinals Live Updates: Diana Shnaider and Maja Chwalinska are currently locking horns in the second women's singles semif-final. Earlier, Mirra Andreeva beat Marta Kostyuk in straight sets to reach her maiden Grand Slam final.

Rohit Sharma doubtful for Afghanistan ODIs after Kohli setback India are awaiting Rohit Sharma's fitness clearance for the Afghanistan ODI series after Virat Kohli was ruled out. The uncertainty has left selectors weighing replacement plans, with Yashasvi Jaiswal in contention and Hardik Pandya also under watch.

Did Dawood Ibrahim plot IPL takeover? Lalit Modi's bombshell claims Former IPL chief Lalit Modi has claimed that a plot by Dawood Ibrahim to control a tournament franchise and threats over disrupted illegal betting networks ultimately forced his complete exile from cricket administration.

Gaikwad likely to replace Kohli for Afghanistan ODI series Virat Kohli has been ruled out of the Afghanistan ODI series with a hamstring injury from the IPL 2026 final. Ruturaj Gaikwad is the leading candidate to replace him as selectors reshuffle India's squad.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Rose Bowl, SouthamptonThe Rose Bowl, Hampshire's current home ground, opened its doors in 2001 and represented a major step forward for the county's cricket infrastructure. Designed by renowned architects Michael Hopkins & Partners, the venue is known for its bowl-shaped layout and striking three-tier pavilion, which has become a defining feature of the stadium.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Old Trafford, ManchesterWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Old Trafford in Manchester is one of England's most historic cricket grounds. Home to Lancashire CCC, it has hosted Test matches since 1884 and is known for its electric atmosphere. Old Trafford can accommodate up to 26,000 spectators.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Headingley, LeedsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Headingley is one of England's most historic cricket grounds, famed for the 1981 Ashes miracle and generations of iconic performances. The Leeds venue remains a stage for unforgettable cricket moments.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Venue: Edgbaston, BirminghamWomen's T20 World Cup 2026 venue: Edgbaston is widely regarded as one of cricket's premier venues and has served as the home ground of Warwickshire County Cricket Club since 1885.

Women's T20 World Cup 2026 venue: Lord's, LondonLord's will stage key Women's T20 World Cup matches, including the final in London. The venue's history, slope and intense atmosphere add extra weight to every contest.

How Gill became India's new Test run machine: Ashwin reveals technique tweakAshwin said Shubman Gill's tighter bat-pad alignment has transformed his Test batting. He believes the change can define India's batting line-up for years and score more runs at home.

Sai Sudharsan vs Devdutt Padikkal: India's pick for No.3 to get a long ropeIND vs AFG: India's search for a permanent Test No. 3 continues. Coach Ryan ten Doeschate promises a long rope for Devdutt Padikkal or Sai Sudharsan ahead of the Afghanistan clash.

Rishabh Pant doesn't need a title to lead, he is in great spirits: India coachRyan ten Doeschate said Rishabh Pant remains upbeat and influential despite losing India's vice-captaincy for the Afghanistan Test. India will play the one-off Test in New Chandigarh from June 6.

Ayush Shetty falls to Lee Chiuk Yiu as India crash out of Indonesia OpenAyush Shetty lost to Lee Chiuk Yiu in three games, ending India's last live run at the Indonesia Open. The defeat completed a poor week for the Indian big names, with Sindhu beaten, others out early and Sat-Chi withdrawing injured.

No Ashwin or Jadeja but Kuldeep backs India's new spin unit for Mullanpur TestIND vs AFG: For the first time in 15 years, India will play a home Test without Ashwin or Jadeja. Senior spinner Kuldeep Yadav backs a fresh, young spin unit to step up against Afghanistan in Mullanpur.

Legacy on the Line: 6 stars who must shine at FIFA World Cup 2026FIFA World Cup 2026: These superstars cannot afford to slip up during the FIFA World Cup 2026 as their teams will depend on them heavily during the course of the competition. Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland will be expected to bring in the goals, while Lamine Yamal and Bruno Fernandes will provide the creative spark for their respective teams.

Group E preview: Can Germany reclaim former glory after successive early exits?FIFA World Cup, Group E preview: Germany may headline Group E, but Ecuador and Ivory Coast have the quality and belief to upset the established order. With World Cup debutants Curacao eager to make history, this promises to be one of the tournament's most competitive and unpredictable groups.

Vinesh Phogat one-ups WFI again as Supreme Court rejects federation's petitionThe Supreme Court dismissed WFI's petition against Vinesh Phogat's participation in the selection trials, calling it infructuous.

French Open Day 11 Schedule: Kostyuk, Andreeva meet amid Ukraine-Russia backdropFrench Open 2026 Day 11 Order of Play: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk and Russia's Mirra Andreeva headline a fascinating French Open semi-final line-up, with all four remaining players chasing a maiden Grand Slam title in Paris.

Manchester United owners Glazer family considering sale of stakes at clubMembers of the Glazer family are discussing whether to sell some or all of their Manchester United shares, according to a report from Bloomberg. The talks could reshape control at Old Trafford after Jim Ratcliffe's investment and years of supporter anger.

Virat Kohli to miss ODI series against Afghanistan due to injuryVirat Kohli will miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to a hamstring injury. Kohli suffered the setback during the IPL 2026 final where he helped RCB win their second consecutive title.

Indonesia Open: Familiar foe An Se Young proves too strong for PV Sindhu yet againPV Sindhu's Indonesia Open campaign ended in the second round as world No. 1 An Se Young claimed a 21-17, 21-15 victory, extending her perfect head-to-head record against the Indian to 10-0.

Are Gambhir and selectors on the same page over India's new T20I captain choice?Gautam Gambhir and the selectors are aligned on moving past Suryakumar Yadav, but not on who should lead India's T20I side next. Gambhir seems to have doubts about Shreyas Iyer as the new captain and has his own preferred choice for the role.

Indonesia Open Highlights: PV Sindhu loses again to An Se Young in 2nd roundPV Sindhu vs An Se Young Highlights, Indonesia Open: An Se Young extended her dominance over PV Sindhu with a 21-17, 21-15 victory. The Korean claimed her 10th straight win over the Indian, sealing the match in just two games.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
